title: cacalia santacruzensis
intro: Cacalia santacruzensis, also known as the Santa Cruz groundsel, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the daisy family, Asteraceae. Traditionally, Cacalia santacruzensis has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant's leaves and roots have been used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, as well as to aid in the treatment of skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of Cacalia santacruzensis, such as infusions and decoctions, are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
